title: Marmite Butter Beans with Butternut Squash, Soft Eggs & Cottage Cheese
summary: Creamy Marmite beans with tender squash, soft eggs, and cottage cheese. Cosy, savoury, and packed with umami.

method.description: Peel the butternut squash and cut into 2cm chunks. Add to a large nonstick deep frying pan with water over a medium heat. Simmer, covered, for 15 mins, topping up with splashes of water if needed, until the squash is nearly tender but still has a bit of bite.
Strip the cavolo nero leaves from their stems and tear into large pieces. Add to the pan for the final 5 mins of squash cooking time, so it softens but stays vibrant.
Meanwhile, bring a pan of water to the boil. Lower in the eggs and boil for 7 mins for soft centres. Run them under cold water until cool enough to handle, then peel and set aside.
Tip the butter beans (with their liquid) into the pan. Simmer, covered, for another 5 mins until the squash is very tender.
Add the Marmite to the pan and gently stir it through. Grate in half the Parmesan and stir to melt.
Spoon the beans into bowls. Halve the eggs and place on top. Add dollops of cottage cheese and finish with the rest of the Parmesan. Season with black pepper and a drizzle of olive oil, if you like.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
